summary.verified_gap: no XHTTP/WS fallback on public standard HTTPS 443

recommended_path.title: Keep current NL Reality 443 alive during preparation
recommended_path.reason: public 443 currently serves existing Reality clients

recommended_path.title: Prepare an independent public HTTPS 443 fallback endpoint
recommended_path.reason: this avoids moving the current NL 443 listener before replacement is proven

recommended_path.title: Canary with one admin client before subscription promotion
Promote HTTPS 443 fallback first only after canary passes

rejected_shortcuts: move NL public 443 from Reality to nginx without tested replacement
store raw VPN URIs or UUIDs in local reports

allowed_before_confirm: public TCP probe of a non-secret candidate endpoint
forbidden_before_confirm: change bot subscription delivery on NL

acceptance_criteria: public HTTPS 443 fallback endpoint exists and is not the current raw Reality listener
XHTTP and WebSocket dataplane tests pass through public 443
subscription output contains xhttp:443 before legacy Reality entries
at least one work/restricted Wi-Fi client passes
at least one mobile network client passes
rollback keeps the old NL Reality 443 profile available

gates.title: Current NL 443 owner is known
gates.next_step: refresh the read-only NL profile before any future staging

gates.title: Existing fallback is on 8443, not standard 443
gates.next_step: stop treating additional port tries as the main fix

gates.title: User reports every configured port failed
gates.next_step: treat the work-network cause as unverified but stop relying on current ports

gates.title: In-place 443 move would affect existing Reality users
gates.next_step: do not move public 443 from Reality to nginx until a tested replacement path exists

gates.title: Independent HTTPS 443 endpoint is the low-blast-radius path
gates.next_step: provision and validate a non-NL/non-SPB endpoint with public HTTPS 443 before user migration
